UI/UX AUDIT REPORT: OCBC Dual Currency Investment (DCI)

Comprehensive UI/UX Audit Report
Audit Date
2026-04-20
Platform
Web desktop (1280px+)
Auditor Persona
N/A
Flow Scope
End-to-end DCI investment flow — landing page, investment configuration, account selection, review, confirmation, post-investment monitoring, reinvestment, and prerequisite account flows.
N/A
UX Health Score / 10

Executive Summary

4
Critical
7
High
11
Medium
3
Low

All Findings (25)

#Screen / ComponentDimensionSeverityFindingRecommendation
#1 Landing — Hero Banner & Value Props 9. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL Landing page copy exclusively promotes upside — 'Ready to invest?', 'Turn your foreign currency needs into potential for better returns', 'Maximise your investment returns', 'higher than interest rates on an ordinary time deposit.' Zero risk mention for a capital-at-risk structured product. Add mandatory risk disclaimer below hero: 'Important: DCI is a structured investment product, not a deposit. Your principal may be converted to the alternate currency at maturity. Capital is at risk. Not covered by deposit insurance.'
#2 Entire Flow (Config → Accounts → Review → Submit) 9. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL Risk disclosures exist only in collapsed FAQ on landing page. User can configure, select accounts, review, and submit — committing real capital — without encountering any risk warning or acknowledgment step. Insert risk summary card at top of Configuration screen. Add mandatory risk acknowledgment checkbox on Review screen before Submit is enabled.
#3 Pre-Configuration (No gate) 9. Trust & Emotional Design CRITICAL No suitability assessment or Customer Knowledge Assessment (CKA) before allowing investment in a structured product with embedded derivatives. Any authenticated corporate user can immediately invest. Add suitability gate before configuration: 3-4 questions confirming user understands DCI is not a deposit, settlement may be in alternate currency, and principal is at risk. For returning users, allow annual re-certification bypass.
#4 Review — Submit Section 6. Forms & Data Entry CRITICAL No explicit T&C consent checkbox. Only implicit consent via small-print text: 'By clicking Submit, you confirm that you have read, understood and agree to the Terms and Conditions.' Insufficient for structured products with embedded derivatives. Add mandatory checkboxes: '☐ I have read and agree to the Terms and Conditions' and '☐ I understand this investment may settle in the alternate currency and my principal is at risk.' Both required before Submit enables.
#5 Config — Strike Price Selection 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH Annualized yield (e.g. 6.50% p.a.) is the primary decision anchor and only yield format shown. For 1-month tenor, actual return is ~0.54%; for 1-week, ~0.125%. Actual period return never displayed. Show both formats: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)' or 'Est. return: ~$1,083 USD'. Display absolute dollar return prominently in the Potential Outcomes panel.
#6 Config — Strike Price Selection 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH Strike price list shows yield-risk tradeoff as purely better/worse yields. Higher yield = greater probability of alternate currency settlement, but this relationship is unexplained. Add explanatory note above strike prices: 'Strike prices closer to the current spot rate offer higher yields but have a greater chance of your investment settling in the alternate currency.'
#7 Config — Investment Amount Field 5. Component & Interaction Design HIGH Minimum amount helper text displays '$0,000.00 USD' — template placeholder never replaced with actual minimum. Replace with actual dynamic minimum (e.g., 'Minimum: 50,000.00 USD'). Make dynamic per currency pair.
#8 Config — All Financial Terms 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH Financial jargon without inline definitions or tooltips: Tenor, Strike Price, Currency pair, Spot Rate, Fixing Rate, Base Currency, Alternate Currency. ⓘ icons exist but tooltip content not visible in design. Ensure ⓘ tooltip content is designed. Use plain language alongside jargon: 'Tenor (Investment Period)', 'Strike Price (Target Exchange Rate)'. Add definitions for every ⓘ icon.
#9 Config — Scenario Panels 4. Colour & Contrast HIGH Scenario A (Base Currency) uses green; Scenario B (Alternate Currency) uses peach/orange. Creates unconscious bias: green = good, orange = bad. Either scenario could be preferred depending on user's currency needs. Use neutral colours for both scenarios (e.g., light blue and light grey). Or allow user to declare preferred outcome and highlight accordingly.
#10 Reinvest Config — Strike Not Met 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity HIGH After strike-not-met settlement, reinvestment screen silently flips currency pair (e.g., EUR/SGD → SGD/EUR). Base currency changes without explanation. Add contextual banner: 'Your previous investment settled in SGD. This reinvestment uses SGD as your base currency.' Show original vs new pair.
#11 Config → Accounts → Review 1. IA & Navigation HIGH No step progress indicator across the 3-step investment flow. Users cannot gauge remaining steps or their position in the flow. Add horizontal stepper: '1. Configure → 2. Accounts → 3. Review → Confirmation'. Persist across all steps.
#12 Config — Spot Rate & Yield 7. Feedback & System Status MEDIUM 'Indicative' qualifier on spot rate and yield unexplained. Users don't know if rates are guaranteed, estimated, or may change before submission. Add tooltip: 'Indicative rates based on current market conditions; may change. Final rate confirmed at submission.'
#13 Review — Full Page 2. Visual Hierarchy & Layout MEDIUM Review screen requires scrolling through 5 sections before Submit button. No sticky CTA bar, floating summary, or collapsible sections. ~3x viewport height. Add sticky bottom bar with key summary (Amount, Pair, Yield) + Submit. Or make sections collapsible.
#14 T&C Modal 3. Typography & Readability MEDIUM T&C presented as undifferentiated wall of text — no headings, bullets, numbered clauses, or section breaks. Users cannot scan for relevant clauses. Structure with section headings, numbered clauses, bullets. Highlight 3-5 key risk clauses visually. Add table of contents.
#15 Confirmation — CTA Priority 9. Trust & Emotional Design MEDIUM 'Place Another Investment' is primary CTA (filled dark button) immediately after placing investment. Nudges impulsive repeat investment without reflection. Make 'Back to Dual Currency Investment' the primary CTA. Move 'Place Another Investment' to secondary (outline button).
#16 Confirmation — Expanded vs Collapsed 8. Cognitive Load & Clarity MEDIUM Expanded confirmation repeats nearly all review information. At confirmation, users need reassurance + ref number, not a full recap. Default to collapsed confirmation (summary + ref number + timeline). Provide expandable 'View full details' for opt-in.
#17 Investment Details — Expiring Soon 7. Feedback & System Status MEDIUM 'Expiring Soon' detail screen shows no settlement outcome section, no Potential Outcomes panel, no projected scenarios. Users approaching expiry have less information than at placement. Add 'Projected Outcome' section showing current spot rate vs strike price. Show countdown to fixing date/time.
#18 Landing — Expiring Soon Status 4. Colour & Contrast MEDIUM 'Expiring soon' status badge uses red/orange text. Red in banking = error/danger, but expiry is a normal lifecycle event. Use neutral amber badge. Reserve red for errors/actions required. Consider 'Approaching Maturity' as clearer label.
#19 Landing — Value Proposition Cards 2. Visual Hierarchy & Layout MEDIUM Three value cards present benefits without balanced risk context. Icons are generic and don't clearly relate to text content. Balance at least one card with risk context. Replace generic icons with DCI-specific illustrations.
#20 Account Selection — Page Layout 2. Visual Hierarchy & Layout MEDIUM Account selection uses <40% viewport width. Large whitespace on right side. No contextual information about why two settlement accounts are needed. Add right-side panel explaining why two settlement accounts are needed with scenario connection.
#21 Config — Currency Selector 5. Component & Interaction Design MEDIUM Investment Amount currency selector shows 'N.A' as default state with generic flag icon. Non-standard and confusing. Change default to 'Select Currency' or pre-populate with user's primary account currency.
#22 Landing — FAQ Expansion 3. Typography & Readability MEDIUM FAQ contains excellent DCI education (definitions, worked examples, risk disclosures, payoff tables) but is buried below fold, behind accordion toggles, on landing page only. Not accessible during investment flow. Surface key FAQ content contextually throughout the flow. Make FAQ searchable.
#23 Post-Investment — Table Column Density 2. Visual Hierarchy & Layout LOW Active Investments table packs 7 columns plus inline settlement banners. May cause horizontal scrolling on narrower viewports. Consider progressive disclosure: 5 key columns in table, full detail on row click. Responsive table pattern for narrower viewports.
#24 All Screens — Yield 'p.a.' Label 3. Typography & Readability LOW 'p.a.' abbreviation never expanded to 'per annum' or 'per year' anywhere in the flow. Expand on first use: '6.50% p.a. (per year)'. Use 'annual' in full where space permits.
#25 Confirmation — Timeline Dates 7. Feedback & System Status LOW Confirmation shows 2-day gap between Trade Date and Start Date with no explanation of T+2 settlement convention. Add brief note: 'Your investment starts 2 business days after placement (standard settlement).'

Annotated Screenshots

Each screenshot is annotated with numbered markers corresponding to findings above. Markers are colour-coded by severity.

Account Selection

Findings on this screen:

#20 MEDIUM Account selection uses <40% viewport width. Large whitespace on right side. No contextual information about why two settlement accounts are needed.
→ Add right-side panel explaining why two settlement accounts are needed with scenario connection.

Active Investments

Findings on this screen:

#18 MEDIUM 'Expiring soon' status badge uses red/orange text. Red in banking = error/danger, but expiry is a normal lifecycle event.
→ Use neutral amber badge. Reserve red for errors/actions required. Consider 'Approaching Maturity' as clearer label.
#23 LOW Active Investments table packs 7 columns plus inline settlement banners. May cause horizontal scrolling on narrower viewports.
→ Consider progressive disclosure: 5 key columns in table, full detail on row click. Responsive table pattern for narrower viewports.

Config Empty

Findings on this screen:

#2 CRITICAL Risk disclosures exist only in collapsed FAQ on landing page. User can configure, select accounts, review, and submit — committing real capital — without encountering any risk warning or acknowledgment step.
→ Insert risk summary card at top of Configuration screen. Add mandatory risk acknowledgment checkbox on Review screen before Submit is enabled.
#7 HIGH Minimum amount helper text displays '$0,000.00 USD' — template placeholder never replaced with actual minimum.
→ Replace with actual dynamic minimum (e.g., 'Minimum: 50,000.00 USD'). Make dynamic per currency pair.
#8 HIGH Financial jargon without inline definitions or tooltips: Tenor, Strike Price, Currency pair, Spot Rate, Fixing Rate, Base Currency, Alternate Currency. ⓘ icons exist but tooltip content not visible in design.
→ Ensure ⓘ tooltip content is designed. Use plain language alongside jargon: 'Tenor (Investment Period)', 'Strike Price (Target Exchange Rate)'. Add definitions for every ⓘ icon.
#11 HIGH No step progress indicator across the 3-step investment flow. Users cannot gauge remaining steps or their position in the flow.
→ Add horizontal stepper: '1. Configure → 2. Accounts → 3. Review → Confirmation'. Persist across all steps.

Config Filled

Findings on this screen:

#5 HIGH Annualized yield (e.g. 6.50% p.a.) is the primary decision anchor and only yield format shown. For 1-month tenor, actual return is ~0.54%; for 1-week, ~0.125%. Actual period return never displayed.
→ Show both formats: '6.50% p.a. (~0.54% for 1 month)' or 'Est. return: ~$1,083 USD'. Display absolute dollar return prominently in the Potential Outcomes panel.
#6 HIGH Strike price list shows yield-risk tradeoff as purely better/worse yields. Higher yield = greater probability of alternate currency settlement, but this relationship is unexplained.
→ Add explanatory note above strike prices: 'Strike prices closer to the current spot rate offer higher yields but have a greater chance of your investment settling in the alternate currency.'
#9 HIGH Scenario A (Base Currency) uses green; Scenario B (Alternate Currency) uses peach/orange. Creates unconscious bias: green = good, orange = bad. Either scenario could be preferred depending on user's currency needs.
→ Use neutral colours for both scenarios (e.g., light blue and light grey). Or allow user to declare preferred outcome and highlight accordingly.
#12 MEDIUM 'Indicative' qualifier on spot rate and yield unexplained. Users don't know if rates are guaranteed, estimated, or may change before submission.
→ Add tooltip: 'Indicative rates based on current market conditions; may change. Final rate confirmed at submission.'
#24 LOW 'p.a.' abbreviation never expanded to 'per annum' or 'per year' anywhere in the flow.
→ Expand on first use: '6.50% p.a. (per year)'. Use 'annual' in full where space permits.

#15 MEDIUM 'Place Another Investment' is primary CTA (filled dark button) immediately after placing investment. Nudges impulsive repeat investment without reflection.
→ Make 'Back to Dual Currency Investment' the primary CTA. Move 'Place Another Investment' to secondary (outline button).

Landing

Findings on this screen:

#1 CRITICAL Landing page copy exclusively promotes upside — 'Ready to invest?', 'Turn your foreign currency needs into potential for better returns', 'Maximise your investment returns', 'higher than interest rates on an ordinary time deposit.' Zero risk mention for a capital-at-risk structured product.
→ Add mandatory risk disclaimer below hero: 'Important: DCI is a structured investment product, not a deposit. Your principal may be converted to the alternate currency at maturity. Capital is at risk. Not covered by deposit insurance.'
#3 CRITICAL No suitability assessment or Customer Knowledge Assessment (CKA) before allowing investment in a structured product with embedded derivatives. Any authenticated corporate user can immediately invest.
→ Add suitability gate before configuration: 3-4 questions confirming user understands DCI is not a deposit, settlement may be in alternate currency, and principal is at risk. For returning users, allow annual re-certification bypass.
#19 MEDIUM Three value cards present benefits without balanced risk context. Icons are generic and don't clearly relate to text content.
→ Balance at least one card with risk context. Replace generic icons with DCI-specific illustrations.

→ Add mandatory checkboxes: '☐ I have read and agree to the Terms and Conditions' and '☐ I understand this investment may settle in the alternate currency and my principal is at risk.' Both required before Submit enables.
